title: AI agent orchestration example — onatopp-dev-norn in Aion
task: Create an agent orchestration example at examples/agent-orchestration/ that demonstrates using Aion to orchestrate AI agent workflows — the exact use case Meridian solves today with Rhai. The workflow accepts a task brief, dispatches it to a 'dev' activity (AI agent writes code), then dispatches a 'review' activity (AI agent reviews the code), and returns the final result. This shows Aion orchestrating multi-step AI agent pipelines with durability — if the dev step takes 30 minutes and the process crashes, it resumes at the review step without re-running the dev step.

requirements.description: Create a Gleam workflow at examples/agent-orchestration/src/orchestrator.gleam using aion_flow. The workflow accepts TaskInput (title, description, requirements as a list of strings), dispatches a 'develop' activity with the full brief, receives DevOutput (code_diff, commit_message), then dispatches a 'review' activity with the brief and dev output, receives ReviewOutput (verdict: 'land' or 'revise', findings as list of strings). If verdict is 'revise', loop back to develop with the findings appended. Max 3 iterations. Return the final DevOutput on land or Error on exhausted iterations.
requirements.acceptance: gleam build succeeds and the workflow correctly implements the dev-review loop with bounded iterations

requirements.description: Create a packager at examples/agent-orchestration/packager/ and build orchestrator.aion.

Create examples/agent-orchestration/worker.py implementing the develop and review activity handlers. For the demo, these should be simulated (no actual AI calls) — develop returns a plausible code diff and commit message based on the title, review returns 'land' on the second attempt (first attempt returns 'revise' with a finding). Log each step clearly so the demo is visual and shows the iteration loop.
requirements.acceptance: Worker serves both activities. The workflow runs through one revision cycle before landing.

requirements.description: Create examples/agent-orchestration/README.md explaining: how Aion replaces fragile script-based agent orchestration (YAML, Rhai) with durable typed workflows, the dev-review loop pattern, build and run steps, what to observe in the event history showing the iteration.
requirements.acceptance: README clearly positions Aion as a durable AI agent orchestrator and the demo is runnable

context: This is the killer market demo — it shows Aion solving the exact problem Meridian faces: orchestrating AI agent workflows with durability. The existing onatopp-dev-norn workflow in Meridian is a Rhai script that dispatches dev and review steps. This Aion version adds: type safety (Gleam), crash recovery (if the dev step takes 30 minutes and crashes, replay returns the cached result), and observability (dashboard shows the iteration loop in the event history). Follow examples/hello-world/ structure. Update dev-config.json workflow_packages to include this example.
